name: Credit points success (member ID)
description: Documented Sample JSON Response (lines 22799-22815) for a successful CreditPointsToMembers run. status=true, message=null, and outputParameters.outputParameters.results contains the credited points. requestExample is the documented member-ID sample request (lines 22751-22768).
notes: IMPLEMENTER-DEFINED: Doc explicitly states (lines 22544-22545, 22744-22745) that "the request and response parameters vary depending on the input and output parameters configured for the CreditPointsToMembers process type in a Salesforce org." So the inner contents of processParameters[] (TransactionJournal sub-fields, PointsToCredit) and outputParameters.outputParameters.results[] (PointsCredited) are org-configurable — only the OUTER envelope is fixed: request top-level "processParameters" array (Loyalty Process Parameter Map, Required, v56.0), and response top-level keys message / outputParameters / simulationDetails / status. processName in the path is the name of a process cloned from the CreditPointsToMembers template; the doc's example URL uses ".../program-processes/creditPoints" (a custom process name) while the resource-examples table uses ".../program-processes/CreditPointsToMembers" (line 22566) — mockPath uses the template name CreditPointsToMembers as the target requested. Available version 56.0 (line 22599); doc example URL uses v67.0 base. No documented error/empty-result fixture exists for this specific process, so only success fixtures are provided (the doc shows two request variants sharing one success response). The "Loyalty Process Output" named schema (line 22633) has no separate property table in this section — its shape is given only via the inline Sample JSON Response. Interchangeable field-name note appears at lines 22749-22750 (e.g. MemberId vs MembershipNumber).
